summary: Written immediately after the 2016 election, this brief call to action urges readers to move past shock and analysis paralysis. O'Neil dismisses post-mortem discussions about polling failures and demographics as distractions. Instead, she advocates for supporting independent journalism and preparing for active civic engagement. The post frames the moment as a turning point where passive citizenship is no longer an option.
key_points: Comfort and self-care are necessary but temporary - action must follow
Analyzing what went wrong (polling, demographics) is a distraction from what comes next
Independent journalism is a critical tool for the coming period
Distinguishing information from propaganda is now a personal responsibility
Civic passivity is no longer viable - everyone must become an activist
money_quotes: Go bake your pie, your lasagna. Get your comfort food made, and check on the kids.
It's time to prepare for whatever the hell is happening next.
It's up to us to focus, to value information over propaganda.
Nobody else is going to do that for us.
Because we are all activists now.
key_insight: In moments of political crisis, the luxury of passive citizenship disappears - distinguishing truth from propaganda becomes everyone's personal responsibility.
